what_it_does: This file is an interpretive analysis record that captures the System Map encoding-grammar spec: how file/folder metadata facets are mapped to visual channels through single-sourced encoding sets stored in a CV_MEANING profile, baked into system-map.json, and projected into the map UI.
observations: Identifies CV_MEANING.encodings('system-map') in assets/icons/cv-meaning.js as the single home for encoding sets.
States that system-map.json receives the baked encoding data and the map HTML projects it rather than copying it.
Defines an encoding set as a facet-to-channel binding that offloads recognition to spatial/visual cognition.

Lists reserved channels: colour for primary anchor, size for sibling-relative magnitude, texture for a second categorical facet, border for depth/state, glow for liveness only, and opacity for de-emphasis.
Enumerates facets: role, ext, roleGroup, links, usedBy, bytes, depth, state, with room for computed extensions.
Provides a JSONC set schema with discrete (values + fallback + optional folderColour) and scale (stops/interpolated) kinds.
Distinguishes live sets (role-colour, type-colour, link-heat, size selectors) from draft:true sets awaiting renderers (depth-border, kind-texture, state-glow).
Notes that size SIZERS currently mirror the encoding sets one-to-one and should eventually resolve from the registry.
Closes with a principle gate: any colour, size, or texture literal written inside system-map.html is drift and should move into the profile.
Frames the work as part of a numbered 'Piece N' living-codebase-canvas series.
standouts: The visual-encoding vocabulary is itself a surface-scoped CV_MEANING profile, so it round-trips to JSON and is authored without code.
Glow is reserved strictly for liveness/attention, showing a deliberate channel budget.
The design explicitly offloads category/magnitude recognition to visual cognition so reasoning can stay on meaning.
New encodings appear in the selector by registration alone, without UI code changes.
conventions: Single-source / one-home discipline: the map holds references, not copies; drift is a literal in the UI.
Extend-by-registration: adding a set entry surfaces it in the selector with no UI edits.
draft:true flag marks data+intent-only sets that lack a channel renderer.
Numbered 'Piece N' framing within the living-codebase-canvas series.
Each channel is reserved for a single semantic purpose (channel budgeting).
concerns: Acknowledged transitional duplication: the size SIZERS mirror the encoding sets one-to-one and are not yet unified to resolve from the registry.
notes: Interpretive snapshot of the System Map visual-encoding grammar spec, describing how metadata facets are bound to visual channels through a single-sourced CV_MEANING profile and projected into the map UI.
questions: Have the draft channels (depth-border, kind-texture, state-glow) been implemented in the map yet, or are they still data-only?
Has the SIZERS/sets unification noted as pending actually happened in the code?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file declares itself a draft/handoff spec for the visual encoding grammar and delivers the channels, facets, schema, current sets, projection wiring, and extension rules.
apparent_themes: Single-source / projection (UI reflects the registry, never parallels it)
Channel budgeting (one visual property per semantic purpose)
intention_signals: Trying to make the codebase-canvas appearance a single-sourced, data-driven grammar of facet-to-channel bindings so categories and magnitudes read at a glance, and so new encodings appear in the UI by registration rather than by editing the map.
novelty: Treating the visual-encoding vocabulary itself as a surface-scoped CV_MEANING profile, and the deliberate per-channel semantic reservation (glow=liveness only, size=sibling-relative magnitude).
contribution: Contributes the channel/facet vocabulary, the discrete-and-scale encoding-set schema, the live-vs-draft set inventory, and the projection-from-registry wiring that a fused system would keep as its codebase-visualization grammar.
summary_for_embedding: Interpretive analysis of the System Map visual-encoding grammar spec defining facet-to-channel bindings single-sourced in a CV_MEANING profile and projected into the map UI.
intention_for_embedding: To document how the codebase canvas's appearance is governed by a data-driven, registry-based encoding grammar rather than hardcoded UI values.
